title: NixOS Android Builder
text:
Preface
The NixOS Android Builder produces signed, ephemeral disk images for building AOSP. Each boot starts clean: the build partition is encrypted with a fresh random key and wiped on shutdown. A Keylime attestation server continuously verifies that builder machines are running unmodified software.

title: NixOS Android Builder
text:
Quick Start
nix run .#create-signing-keys          # generate Secure Boot keys
nix build .#image                       # build the disk image
install -m 600 result/*.raw .           # copy out of the nix store
nix run .#configure-disk-image -- sign --keystore ./keys --device *.raw
sudo dd bs=1M status=progress if=*.raw of=/dev/sdX && sudo sync
Or try it in a VM:
nix run .#run-vm

title: 1  Getting Started
text: 1.1 Requirements

title: 1  Getting Started
text:
A local machine with Nix installed, building for x86_64-linux.

~4 GB memory for evaluation
~30 GB disk space for dependencies and images

A USB mass storage device (~3 GB) to transfer the image.
A target machine for building Android:

~64 GB memory
~250 GB free disk space
UEFI with Secure Boot in Setup Mode

A git checkout of this repository. Commands assume the repository root unless stated otherwise.
(Optional) An attestation server running Keylime. See Chapter 7. Not required for Chapter 6.

title: 1  Getting Started
text:
1.2 Generate Secure Boot Keys
The create-signing-keys helper generates a PK/KEK/DB key set in keys/:
nix run .#create-signing-keys
These keys are required for Secure Boot signing and enrollment. They must not be committed plain-text. Tools such as SOPS can manage them securely.

title: 2  Building & Signing Images
text:
2.1 Build
Build the disk image. Dependencies are fetched from cache.nixos.org; subsequent runs are cached.
The result is a raw disk image (~2.7 GB) with a full partition table and NixOS closure.

title: 2  Building & Signing Images
text:
nix build --print-build-logs .#image






Tip



nix-output-monitor gives better build progress. Install with nix shell nixpkgs#nom, then use nom build instead.

title: 2  Building & Signing Images
text:
2.2 Sign
configure-disk-image sign signs the UKI and copies Secure Boot enrollment bundles. It runs outside the Nix sandbox to avoid leaking keys into /nix/store.
Copy the image out of the read-only store, then sign:
install -m 600 result/*.raw .
nix run .#configure-disk-image -- sign --keystore ./keys --device *.raw

title: 2  Building & Signing Images
text:
2.3 Configure Attestation Server
The image needs to know how to reach the attestation server. This writes the server address and CA certificate to the ESP:
nix run .#configure-disk-image -- set-attestation-server \
    --ip 10.0.0.1 \
    --ca-cert /path/to/ca-cert.pem \
    --device *.raw
If skipped, the keylime agent will fail to start on boot. See Section 8.8 for the JSON schema.
See Chapter 7 for deploying the server itself.

title: 2  Building & Signing Images
text:
2.4 Flash
Flash the signed image to a USB stick or external drive:
sudo dd bs=1M status=progress if=*.raw of=/dev/sdX
sudo sync
Use lsblk to identify the target device.
Enable Secure Boot Setup Mode in the target machine’s firmware before first boot. This is needed for automatic key enrollment. After enrollment, Setup Mode is not needed again unless keys are rotated.

title: 3  Disk Installer
text:
3.1 Build & Sign
The installer image contains a minimal ESP plus the builder image as payload. It boots and flashes the payload to a local disk.
The sign sub-command accepts flags:

title: 3  Disk Installer
text:
nix build -L .#installer-image
install -m 600 result/disk-installer.raw .
nix run .#configure-disk-image -- sign --keystore ./keys --device disk-installer.raw


--installer — sign only the installer UKI
--payload — sign only the payload UKI
--no-auto-enroll — skip copying Secure Boot enrollment bundles

title: 3  Disk Installer
text:
3.2 Configure Install Target
Interactive (user selects disk at boot):
nix run .#configure-disk-image -- set-target --target select --device disk-installer.raw
Automatic (no prompt):
nix run .#configure-disk-image -- set-target --target /dev/vda --device disk-installer.raw

title: 3  Disk Installer
text:
3.3 Verify Configuration
nix run .#configure-disk-image -- status --keystore ./keys --device disk-installer.raw

title: 3  Disk Installer
text:
3.4 Test in a VM
nix run .#installer-vm

title: 4  Running Android Builds
text:
4.1 Fetch Source
After booting, you get a shell with the Android build toolchain.
The scripts below (fetch-android, build-android, etc.) are reference implementations shipped as convenience wrappers. They demonstrate the intended workflow but can be replaced or extended for specific distribution needs.
Uses repo to clone AOSP to /var/lib/build/source. If multiple branches are configured via nixosAndroidBuilder.build.branches, select-branch presents a menu on boot.
Run fetch-android --help for flags (manifest URL, branch, source directory, git identity).

title: 4  Running Android Builds
text: fetch-android

title: 4  Running Android Builds
text:
4.2 Build
build-android
Run build-android --help for flags (lunch target, source directory).
Lower-level tools (lunch, m, ninja) are available after loading Android’s envsetup.sh:
cd /var/lib/build/source
source build/envsetup.sh
See upstream documentation for details.

title: 4  Running Android Builds
text:
4.3 SBOM Generation
android-sbom
Generates a Software Bill of Materials in /var/lib/build/source/out/soong/sbom using upstream SBOM facilities.

title: 4  Running Android Builds
text:
4.4 Source Measurement
android-measure-source
Hashes all files across all git repositories in the checkout, producing out/source_measurement.txt.

title: 4  Running Android Builds
text:
4.5 Save Build Outputs
Outputs are in /var/lib/build/source/out — images in out/target/product, logs in error.log and verbose.log.gz.
Since /var/lib/build is ephemeral, outputs are lost on shutdown. To persist them, set nixosAndroidBuilder.artifactStorage.enable = true. During boot, a second disk is selected for persistent storage at /var/lib/artifacts. It can also be pre-configured:
nix run .#configure-disk-image -- set-storage --target /dev/sdb --device *.raw
The copy-android-outputs script copies outputs matching nixosAndroidBuilder.artifactStorage.contents to /var/lib/artifacts.

title: 4  Running Android Builds
text:
4.6 Credential Storage
A TPM-backed credential store persists secrets across reboots (see Section 13.6).
echo 'my-secret-token' | credential-store add api-token
credential-store list
credential-store show api-token
credential-store remove api-token
Credentials can also be added from a file:
credential-store add api-token ~/token.txt
Credentials are bound to PCR 7 (Secure Boot policy) by default. See Chapter 14 for customization (e.g. adding PCR 11 binding).

title: 4  Running Android Builds
text:
4.7 Inspect PCR State
TPM PCR values can be read from sysfs:
cat /sys/class/tpm/tpm0/pcr-sha256/7
On boot, report-measured-boot-state sends the measured boot reference state to the attestation server automatically. For debugging:
measure-boot-state generates the reference state from the UEFI event log:
measure-boot-state -o /tmp/refstate.json
debug-measured-boot-state diagnoses attestation failures:
debug-measured-boot-state save                    # before reboot
debug-measured-boot-state                         # after reboot — auto-detects saved state
debug-measured-boot-state diagnose -r enrolled.json  # compare against enrolled state

title: 4  Running Android Builds
text:
4.8 Reset to Initial State
Reboot. The ephemeral encryption key is lost, rendering all build data inaccessible. The /var/lib/build partition is reformatted on next boot.
systemctl reboot

title: 4  Running Android Builds
text:
4.9 Updates
Update all dependencies by updating the pinned nixpkgs:
nix flake update
Rebuild the image and run tests (see Chapter 18) to catch regressions.

title: 5  Desktop Variant
text:
5.1 Prerequisites
A persistent NixOS workstation with optional GNOME, intended for physical hardware.

title: 5  Desktop Variant
text:
Nix installed on your local machine
Git checkout of this repository
USB stick (≥ 4 GB)
Target machine with UEFI, Secure Boot support, and a disk to install to

title: 5  Desktop Variant
text:
5.2 Build
Shell (minimal) desktop:
nix build -L .#desktop-installer-image
install -m 600 result/disk-installer.raw desktop-installer.raw
GNOME desktop:
nix build -L .#desktop-gnome-installer-image
install -m 600 result/disk-installer.raw desktop-installer.raw

title: 5  Desktop Variant
text:
5.3 Customise
Edit desktop-configuration.nix:

Set YubiKey public keys in yubikeys.groupA (or clear for password auth)
Set nixosAndroidBuilder.debug = false for production

title: 5  Desktop Variant
text:
5.4 Sign & Flash
nix run .#configure-disk-image -- sign --keystore ./keys --device desktop-installer.raw
nix run .#configure-disk-image -- status --keystore ./keys --device desktop-installer.raw
sudo dd bs=1M status=progress if=desktop-installer.raw of=/dev/sdX
sudo sync

Both “Installer” and “Payload” show “✓ Signed and verified”

title: 5  Desktop Variant
text:
5.5 Prepare Target UEFI

Enable Secure Boot
Put Secure Boot into Setup Mode (clear existing keys)
Set USB as first boot device
Save and reboot

title: 5  Desktop Variant
text:
5.6 Install

Boot from USB — installer appears on tty2
Select target disk (or auto-installs if pre-configured)
Wait for copy to complete
Remove USB when prompted, press Enter to reboot

title: 5  Desktop Variant
text:
5.7 First Boot
Two automatic reboots:

Boot 1: Secure Boot keys auto-enroll → automatic reboot
Boot 2: systemd-repart grows root partition → normal boot

title: 5  Desktop Variant
text:
5.8 Verify

tuigreet login screen appears
bootctl status shows Secure Boot: enabled (user)
df -h / shows root using the full disk
Network is up (ip addr)
Data persists across systemctl reboot

title: 6  Virtual Machine Testing
text:
6.1 Builder VM
Test the full setup in QEMU. Requires KVM (enable VT-x/AMD-V in firmware; verify with lsmod | grep kvm).
Creates a writable image copy, signs it with test keys, and starts QEMU with Secure Boot and TPM. If attestation-server.json is present in the current directory, the keylime agent is configured from it (same format as /boot/attestation-server.json, see Section 8.8).
Subsequent runs reuse the existing .raw file. Delete it to start from a clean image.

title: 6  Virtual Machine Testing
text: nix run .#run-vm

title: 6  Virtual Machine Testing
text:
6.2 Installer VM
nix run .#installer-vm
Tests the Chapter 3 workflow in a VM.

title: 6  Virtual Machine Testing
text:
6.3 Exiting
systemctl poweroff   # from within the VM
Or close the QEMU window.

title: 7  Attestation Server Setup
text:
7.1 What It Runs
The system-manager/ directory turns a stock Linux machine (Ubuntu, Debian, etc.) into a Keylime attestation server — registrar, verifier, auto-enrollment daemon, and an optional attestation-gated git server.
System-manager applies NixOS-style module configurations to non-NixOS hosts without replacing the OS.
See Chapter 8 for ports, data flow, and PKI details.

title: 7  Attestation Server Setup
text:
keylime-registrar — Accepts agent registrations (EK + AK identity)
keylime-verifier — Continuously attests enrolled agents via TPM quotes
keylime-auto-enroll — Bridges registration and verification; auto-enrolls agents
keylime-tls — Oneshot service; auto-generates a self-signed CA and mTLS PKI
keylime-git-nginx / keylime-git-auth (optional) — Attestation-gated git server (see Chapter 9)

title: 7  Attestation Server Setup
text:
7.2 System Users & File Locations

keylime user — runs all services, owns /var/lib/keylime
tss group — TPM device access (/dev/tpmrm0)
/var/lib/keylime/ — Keylime state (SQLite databases)
/var/lib/keylime/tls/ — Auto-generated TLS PKI
/etc/keylime/ — Configuration files (registrar.conf, verifier.conf, tenant.conf, ca.conf, logging.conf)

title: 7  Attestation Server Setup
7  Attestation Server Setup
text:
7.4 Prerequisites

A local machine with Nix installed (flakes enabled)
A target Linux x86_64 machine reachable via SSH as root
Network access from agents to the target on ports 8891, 8881, 8893

Nix does not need to be installed on the target.

title: 7  Attestation Server Setup
text:
7.5 Deploy
git clone <repository-url> nixos-android-builder
cd nixos-android-builder
nix run 'github:numtide/system-manager' -- \
  --target-host root@<server-ip> switch --flake .#default
Activation creates the keylime/tss users, writes config to /etc/keylime/, generates the TLS PKI, and starts all services.

title: 7  Attestation Server Setup
text:
7.6 Verify
systemctl status keylime-tls keylime-registrar keylime-verifier keylime-auto-enroll
ls -la /var/lib/keylime/tls/       # TLS certificates
curl -k https://127.0.0.1:8891/v2.5/agents/   # expect mTLS error — registrar is up

title: 7  Attestation Server Setup
text:
7.7 Open Firewall
sudo ufw allow 8891/tcp   # Registrar (mTLS)
sudo ufw allow 8881/tcp   # Verifier (mTLS)
sudo ufw allow 8893/tcp   # Auto-enroll (server TLS, TOFU)
sudo ufw allow 443/tcp    # Git server (mTLS, optional — only if gitServer.enable is set)

title: 7  Attestation Server Setup
text:
7.8 Distribute CA Certificate
Copy /var/lib/keylime/tls/ca-cert.pem from the server. It is baked into agent images via configure-disk-image set-attestation-server (see Chapter 2).

title: 7  Attestation Server Setup
text:
7.9 Update
git pull
nix run 'github:numtide/system-manager' -- \
  --target-host root@<server-ip> switch --flake .#default

title: 7  Attestation Server Setup
text:
7.10 Deactivate
On the target:
/nix/var/nix/profiles/system-manager/bin/deactivate
Stops managed services and removes config. Does not remove /var/lib/keylime state.

title: 8  Attestation Protocol
text:
8.1 Architecture
Ports, data flow, PCR policy, and PKI reference.
graph LR
    subgraph agent["Agent (Builder)"]
        ka["keylime-agent<br/><i>Rust</i>"]
        rmbs["report-measured-<br/>boot-state"]
    end
    subgraph server["Attestation Server"]
        verifier["keylime-verifier :8881"]
        registrar["keylime-registrar :8891"]
        autoenroll["keylime-auto-enroll :8893"]
    end

    ka -- "mTLS" --> registrar
    verifier <-- "mTLS" --> ka
    rmbs -- "server TLS (TOFU)" --> autoenroll




Figure 8.1: Attestation architecture

title: 8  Attestation Protocol
text:
8.2 Ports & Protocols










Port
Service
Direction
Protocol
Purpose




8891
Registrar
Agent → Server
HTTPS (mTLS)
Agent registration (EK + AK identity)


8881
Verifier
Server ↔︎ Agent
HTTPS (mTLS)
TPM quote requests, enrollment via keylime_tenant


8893
Auto-enroll
Agent → Server
HTTPS (server TLS, TOFU)
Agent POSTs measured boot reference state


443
Git server (nginx)
Agent → Server
HTTPS (mTLS)
Attestation-gated git clone (optional)


8895
Git auth
localhost
HTTP
Auth subrequest from nginx to keylime-git-auth (internal)

title: 8  Attestation Protocol
text:
8.3 Data Exchanged
Port 8891 — Registrar: Agent POSTs its EK, AK, and UUID (SHA-256 of EK) on first boot. Stored in SQLite.
Port 8881 — Verifier: Accepts enrollment (keylime_tenant -c add with --mb_refstate and uki policy). Periodically sends nonces, receives TPM quotes + event logs, replays through the policy.
Port 8893 — Auto-enroll: Agent POSTs {"uuid": "...", "measured_boot_state": {...}}. Daemon matches against registrar entries and auto-enrolls.

title: 8  Attestation Protocol
text:
8.4 Full Boot & Attestation Sequence






sequenceDiagram
    participant A as Agent
    participant R as Registrar :8891
    participant AE as Auto-Enroll :8893
    participant V as Verifier :8881

    Note over A: 1. UEFI measures firmware,<br/>SB keys, UKI into PCRs 0–11
    Note over A: 2. systemd-pcrphase extends PCR 11

    A->>R: 3. Register (EK + AK + UUID)
    R-->>R: 4. Store identity

    A->>AE: 5. POST measured boot refstate
    AE-->>AE: 6. Store report by UUID

    loop Poll interval
        AE->>R: 7a. List registered agents
        AE->>V: 7b. List enrolled agents
        AE->>V: 7c. keylime_tenant -c add
    end

    V->>A: 8. Nonce + PCR selection
    A->>V: TPM quote + event log
    Note over V: 9. Validate: AK signature,<br/>nonce, event log replay,<br/>PCR match (0,1,2,3,4,5,7)




Figure 8.2: Boot and attestation sequence

title: 8  Attestation Protocol
text:
8.5 Measured Boot Reference State
Generated by measure-boot-state from the UEFI binary event log:



Field
Source PCR
Pins




scrtm_and_bios
0
SCRTM version + firmware blob digests


pk
7
Platform Key certificate(s)


kek
7
Key Exchange Key certificate(s)


db
7
Allowed Signature Database entries


dbx
7
Forbidden Signature Database entries


uki_digest
4
SHA-256 of the booted UKI binary

title: 8  Attestation Protocol
text:
8.6 PCR Validation Policy








PCR
Content
Policy




0
SCRTM, firmware blobs
Pinned to refstate


1
BIOS settings, boot variables
Accepted


2
Boot services drivers
Accepted


3
Separator
Separator check


4
UKI application digest
Pinned to refstate


5
GPT partition table
Accepted


7
Secure Boot keys (PK, KEK, db, dbx)
Pinned to refstate


9
systemd-stub event tags
Accepted (tags vary per UKI; NvPCR runtime extension not in UEFI log)


11
UKI PE sections + pcrphase
Accepted (runtime extensions)



Pinned = event log digest must exactly match the enrolled reference state. Accepted = events are syntactically checked but any valid digest is allowed. Separator check = validates the EV_SEPARATOR event marking the boundary between pre-OS and OS-present measurements.
PCRs 9 and 11 are excluded from event-log-vs-TPM replay comparison (they receive runtime extensions not in the UEFI log) but are included in the TPM quote.

title: 8  Attestation Protocol
text:
8.7 TLS / PKI
Auto-generated on the server by keylime-tls.service. Stored in /var/lib/keylime/tls/:








File
Used by
Purpose




ca-cert.pem
All
Root CA — distributed to agents


server-cert.pem / server-key.pem
Registrar, Verifier, Auto-enroll
Server identity


client-cert.pem / client-key.pem
Verifier, Auto-enroll, Tenant
Client identity for mTLS

title: 8  Attestation Protocol
text:
8.8 Agent Configuration
/boot/attestation-server.json (written to ESP at build time):
{
  "ip": "10.0.0.1",
  "port": 8891,
  "verifier_port": 8881,
  "ca_cert": "-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----\n...\n-----END CERTIFICATE-----\n"
}
At boot, keylime-configure-agent reads this and patches /run/keylime/agent.conf.

title: 9  Attestation-Gated Git Server
text:
9.1 How It Works
The git server lets attested builder machines clone repositories over HTTPS/mTLS. A machine that fails attestation, or has never been attested, cannot clone.
sequenceDiagram
    participant A as Agent
    participant N as nginx :443
    participant G as keylime-git-auth :8895
    participant V as keylime-verifier :8881

    A->>N: git clone (mTLS client cert)
    N->>N: ssl_verify_client<br/>(keylime CA validates cert)
    N->>G: auth_request /verify?uuid=…
    G->>V: GET /v2.5/agents/{uuid}
    alt attested (operational state allowed)
        V-->>G: 200
        G-->>N: 200 ALLOW
        N-->>A: serve repo (static files)
    else not attested / unknown
        V-->>G: 404 or disallowed state
        G-->>N: 403 DENY
        N-->>A: clone rejected
    end




Figure 9.1: Git clone authentication flow
Identity. nginx enforces mTLS against the keylime CA. The agent UUID (SHA-256 of the TPM Endorsement Key) is the CN of the client certificate, so no separate credential is needed: a machine provisioned into keylime automatically has its git identity.
Authorization. keylime-git-auth is a small Python daemon on 127.0.0.1:8895. It queries the keylime verifier for the agent’s operational state and only allows requests from agents in a known-good state (START, SAVED, GET_QUOTE, GET_QUOTE_RETRY, PROVIDE_V, PROVIDE_V_RETRY). Everything else is denied, including states it does not recognise. If the verifier is unreachable, it returns 503 and nginx rejects the clone (fail-closed).
Git transport. nginx serves the bare repo directory as static files (git dumb HTTP). No additional daemons are required. Run git update-server-info after each push, or install the provided post-receive hook, to keep the index files current.

title: 9  Attestation-Gated Git Server
text:
9.2 Managing Repositories
Repositories are bare git repos in /var/lib/keylime-git/repos/.
Create a new repo:
git init --bare /var/lib/keylime-git/repos/my-repo.git
chown -R keylime:keylime /var/lib/keylime-git/repos/my-repo.git
git -C /var/lib/keylime-git/repos/my-repo.git update-server-info
ln -s /etc/keylime-git/hooks/post-receive \
      /var/lib/keylime-git/repos/my-repo.git/hooks/post-receive
Mirror an existing repo:
git clone --bare https://github.com/example/repo.git \
    /var/lib/keylime-git/repos/repo.git
chown -R keylime:keylime /var/lib/keylime-git/repos/repo.git
git -C /var/lib/keylime-git/repos/repo.git update-server-info
ln -s /etc/keylime-git/hooks/post-receive \
      /var/lib/keylime-git/repos/repo.git/hooks/post-receive
The post-receive hook runs git update-server-info automatically after every push so the index files stay current.

title: 9  Attestation-Gated Git Server
text:
9.3 Cloning from an Agent
Builder machines include the keylime-git-clone wrapper, which automatically configures git with the machine’s mTLS credentials:
keylime-git-clone https://<server-ip>/my-repo.git
This is equivalent to running git clone with the client certificate, key, and CA cert flags set. An optional destination directory can be appended:
keylime-git-clone https://<server-ip>/my-repo.git /path/to/dest
The credentials are provisioned automatically:

Client cert & key are written to /run/keylime-git/ by report-measured-boot-state after successful attestation.
CA cert is written to /run/keylime-git/ca-cert.pem by report-measured-boot-state from /boot/attestation-server.json.

Both paths are readable by the user account so keylime-git-clone works without elevated privileges. The key is scoped to this machine’s TPM identity and is only useful when paired with a passing attestation.
Custom cert paths can be passed if needed:
keylime-git-clone --cert=/path/to/cert --key=/path/to/key --ca=/path/to/ca \
    https://<server-ip>/my-repo.git

title: 9  Attestation-Gated Git Server
text:
9.4 Verifying the Gate
Check both services are running:
systemctl status keylime-git-nginx keylime-git-auth
Confirm nginx rejects an unauthenticated request:
curl -k https://127.0.0.1:443/any-repo.git/info/refs
# Expected: 400 No required SSL certificate was sent
Watch the attestation gate in real time:
journalctl -fu keylime-git-auth
# Prints ALLOW / DENY with the agent UUID on every request

title: 9  Attestation-Gated Git Server
text:
9.5 Nix Options








Option
Default
Description




services.keylime.gitServer.enable
false
Enable the git server


services.keylime.gitServer.port
443
nginx HTTPS listen port


services.keylime.gitServer.authPort
8895
Auth daemon localhost port


services.keylime.gitServer.repoDir
/var/lib/keylime-git/repos
Bare repo root

title: 10  Design Principles
text:
10.1 Limitations
A reference implementation and proof-of-concept for offline, SLSA‑compliant AOSP builds. The system is modular — individual components (attestation, FHS environment, credential storage) can be adapted or replaced for specific distribution needs.
Core properties:

title: 10  Design Principles
text:
aarch64 support — only x86_64 with UEFI is implemented.
Artifact uploads — build artifacts can be persisted to a second disk but are not automatically uploaded to remote storage.
Configuration — adapting to custom AOSP distributions requires familiarity with NixOS modules. See Chapter 14.

title: 10  Design Principles
text:
10.2 Technologies

NixOS / nixpkgs — declarative system configuration, reproducible builds
systemd — service orchestration, systemd-repart for disk images, systemd-creds for TPM-encrypted credentials
LUKS + dm-verity — disk encryption (ephemeral + TPM2-bound) and read-only store integrity
erofs — compressed read-only filesystem for the immutable store partition
Keylime — TPM-based remote attestation (Rust agent, Python registrar/verifier)
YubiKey / PAM — hardware-token authentication via pam_u2f
USBGuard — USB device blocking during unattended builds
QEMU — VM-based testing

An SBOM for the builder closure can be generated:
nix run github:tiiuae/sbomnix#sbomnix -- .#nixosConfigurations.nixos.toplevel

title: 10  Design Principles
text:
10.3 Deployment Topology






graph LR
    local["Local Machine<br/><i>nix build, sign</i>"]
    server["Attestation Server<br/><i>registrar, verifier,<br/>auto-enroll, git (opt)</i>"]
    builder["Builder Machine<br/><i>keylime-agent, TPM 2.0</i>"]

    local -- "flash via USB" --> builder
    builder -- "register (EK)" --> server
    server <-- "TPM quotes" --> builder




Figure 10.1: Deployment topology






The local machine builds and signs disk images (requires only Nix).
The attestation server runs keylime services, optionally via system-manager on non-NixOS. See Chapter 7.
The builder machine boots the signed image, registers with the server, and runs AOSP builds.

title: 11  Build Process
text:
11.1 Steps
The disk image is assembled in multiple stages using systemd-repart (twice at build time, once at runtime).

title: 11  Build Process
text:
FHS environment (Section 13.1): A derivation assembles all libraries and binaries into an FHS layout — glibc-vanilla (dynamic linker configured for /lib), bash (with FHS $PATH), and Android build dependencies.
NixOS closure (system.build.toplevel): The full system including boot services, the FHS derivation, and all modules.
Intermediate image (system.build.intermediateImage): First systemd-repart run. The store partition is populated with the NixOS closure. boot and store-verity remain empty.
UKI: dm-verity hashes are calculated from the filled store partition. A Unified Kernel Image is built with kernel, initrd, and usrhash on the kernel command line.
Final image (system.build.finalImage): Second systemd-repart run. The store partition is copied, verity hashes are written, and the UKI is placed on the ESP.
Sign UKI: Outside the Nix sandbox, the UKI is extracted, signed with Secure Boot keys, and written back.
Secure Boot bundles: *.auth files are copied to the ESP for automatic enrollment.
The signed image is ready to flash.

title: 11  Build Process
text:
11.2 Disk Layout
Build-time (immutable):



Partition
Label
Format
Mountpoint




00‑esp
boot
vfat
/boot


10‑store‑verity
store-verity
dm-verity hash
n/a


20‑store
store
erofs
/usr



Runtime (created by systemd-repart on first boot):










Partition
Label
Format
Mountpoint
Lifecycle




31‑credentials
var-lib-credentials
LUKS+ext4 (TPM2)
/var/lib/credentials
Persistent


32‑keylime
var-lib-keylime
LUKS+ext4 (TPM2)
/var/lib/keylime
Persistent


40‑build
var-lib-build
LUKS+ext4 (random key)
/var/lib/build
Ephemeral



The root filesystem (/) is tmpfs (RAM only). There is no boot loader — the UKI is loaded directly by the firmware as an EFI application.

title: 12  Boot Sequence
text: 12.1 Steps

title: 12  Boot Sequence
text:
UEFI boot: Firmware loads the UKI, verifying its signature if Secure Boot is active.
Secure Boot check (ensure-secure-boot-enrollment.service):

Active → continue
Setup Mode → enroll certificates from ESP → reboot
Disabled → halt with error

Ephemeral key (generate-disk-key.service): 64 bytes from /dev/urandom, never stored on disk.
Partition setup (systemd-repart): On first boot, creates all mutable partitions. On subsequent boots, leaves persistent partitions untouched, factory-resets the ephemeral build partition with a fresh key.
Mount: Read-only /usr (dm-verity checked), bind-mounts for /bin and /lib (FHS), tmpfs at /, encrypted partitions for build data, credentials, and keylime state.
Switch to NixOS: Stage 2 boot completes.
Build: In unattended mode (Section 13.4), a configurable step sequence runs automatically. In interactive mode, fetch-android, build-android, and related tools are available at the shell (see Chapter 4).
Outputs are in /var/lib/build/source/out. They are lost on shutdown unless artifact storage is enabled.

title: 13  Components
text:
13.1 Custom FHS Environment
A derivation bundles all declared packages into /bin + /lib, bind-mounted at runtime. A custom glibc configures the dynamic linker for /lib; a bash with forFHSEnv puts /bin in $PATH.
pkgs.buildFHSEnv, nix-ld, and envfs were tried but don’t survive sandboxed bind-mounts without /nix/store.

title: 13  Components
text:
13.2 Android Build Environment
The android-build-env.nix module adds AOSP build dependencies to the FHS environment, plus reference wrapper scripts for the default workflow:

fetch-android — clone the configured repo branch
build-android — set lunch target and build
android-sbom — generate SBOM via upstream facilities
android-measure-source — hash all files for source measurement

title: 13  Components
text:
13.3 YubiKey Authentication
The yubikey-auth.nix module enforces hardware-token authentication via pam_u2f. Two key groups can be configured for dual-approval (two people must each touch their YubiKey). Password auth is disabled entirely.

title: 13  Components
text:
13.4 Unattended Mode
When nixosAndroidBuilder.unattended.enable is set, a systemd service runs a configurable step sequence on tty2 after boot. Steps prefixed with root: run with superuser privileges. Default steps:

root:start-shell-if-yubikey-found — poll for YubiKey, allow operator interrupt
select-branch — choose AOSP branch (if multiple configured)
fetch-android — clone sources
build-android — build the lunch target
android-sbom — generate SBOM
android-measure-source — hash source files
copy-android-outputs — persist build artifacts
root:lock-var-lib-build — unmount build partition, destroy encryption key
root:disable-usb-guard — stop USBGuard, re-authorize USB devices
root:start-shell-and-shutdown — interactive shell, then poweroff

USBGuard blocks USB mass storage during builds to prevent data exfiltration.

title: 13  Components
text:
13.5 Remote Attestation (Keylime)
The Keylime agent is enabled by default on every builder image. It uses the push model — the agent periodically sends evidence to the verifier. Identity is derived from the TPM Endorsement Key; no pre-provisioned credentials are needed.
The agent’s Attestation Key is stored on the persistent /var/lib/keylime partition so re-registrations after reboot use the same key.

13.5.1 Auto-Enrollment
The auto-enrollment daemon automates the agent → verifier enrollment:






---
config:
  theme: neutral
---
graph TB
    subgraph server["Attestation Server"]
        registrar["Registrar :8891"]
        verifier["Verifier :8881"]
        daemon["Auto-Enroll :8893"]
        daemon -- polls --> registrar
        daemon -- enrolls --> verifier
    end

    subgraph agent["Agent"]
        ka["keylime-agent"]
        rmbs["report-measured-\nboot-state"]
    end

    ka -- register --> registrar
    rmbs -- POST refstate --> daemon
    verifier -- attest --> ka




Figure 13.1: Auto-enrollment component diagram





The measured boot reference state is accepted on a trust-on-first-use basis — the agent self-reports before first attestation. After enrollment, the verifier replays every event log and validates every TPM quote, so false reports are caught immediately.


13.5.2 Attestation Policy
The custom uki policy (in packages/keylime-measured-boot-policy/) pins security-critical PCRs (0, 4, 7 — firmware, UKI digest, Secure Boot keys) while accepting expected variability in others. See Section 8.6 for the full validation table.


13.5.3 Tools

attestation-ctl — query enrollment status, remove agents from verifier/registrar
measure-boot-state — parse UEFI event log → reference state JSON
report-measured-boot-state — generate and POST reference state to auto-enrollment
debug-measured-boot-state — diagnose failures: replay event log, compare PCRs, diff refstates

title: 13  Components
text:
13.6 Credential Storage
TPM-backed persistent storage via systemd-creds, bound to PCR 7. Credentials are stored on the /var/lib/credentials LUKS partition and bind-mounted to /run/credstore.encrypted/ for automatic consumption by systemd services with LoadCredentialEncrypted=.
See Chapter 4 for usage of the credential-store command.

title: 13  Components
text:
13.7 Fatal Error Handling
The fatal-error.nix module overrides systemd’s emergency target with a dialog-based message box on tty2. Services write to /run/fatal-error on failure.

title: 13  Components
text:
13.8 Debug Mode
nixosAndroidBuilder.debug adds: unauthenticated emergency shell, extra packages (vim, htop, tmux, git), nix with flakes, empty passwords, passwordless sudo, verbose logging, debug shell on tty3. Not for production.

title: 14  NixOS Options Reference
text:
Configure the builder in configuration.nix (or desktop-configuration.nix for the desktop variant). Some options have run-time equivalents — see fetch-android --help and build-android --help.

nixosAndroidBuilder.artifactStorage.artifactDir

Type: absolute path
Default: "/var/lib/artifacts"
Directory where the persistent artifact storage is mounted.

nixosAndroidBuilder.artifactStorage.contents

Type: list of string
Default: [
  "*"
]
list of files (or patterns) to copy from build outputs

nixosAndroidBuilder.artifactStorage.diskLabel

Type: string
Default: "artifacts"
disk label that identifies the storage partition

nixosAndroidBuilder.artifactStorage.enable

Type: boolean
Default: false
Whether to enable Storing outputs in an unencrypted, persistent disk partition.

nixosAndroidBuilder.build.branches

Type: list of string
Default: [
  "android-latest-release"
]
List of valid branches that can be selected for building. If only one branch is specified, it will be selected automatically. If more than one branch is specified, a dialog menu will be shown for 30 seconds. First entry will be the default if no other one is selected.

nixosAndroidBuilder.build.lunchTarget

Type: string
Default: "aosp_cf_x86_64_only_phone-aosp_current-eng"
Name of lunch target to build. Can be overriden at run-time by passing --lunch-target to build-android.

nixosAndroidBuilder.build.repoManifestUrl

Type: string
Default: "https://android.googlesource.com/platform/manifest"
URL of a repo manifest to fetch sources from. Can be overriden at run-time by passing --repo-manifest-url to fetch-android.

nixosAndroidBuilder.build.sourceDir

Type: absolute path
Default: "/var/lib/build/source"
Directory where repo checkout is stored. Can be overriden at run-time by passing --source-dir to fetch-android and build-android.

nixosAndroidBuilder.build.userEmail

Type: string
Default: "ci@example.com"
Email address used for repo/git operations. Can be overriden at run-time by passing --user-email to fetch-android.

nixosAndroidBuilder.build.userName

Type: string
Default: "CI User"
User name used for repo/git operations. Can be overriden at run-time by passing --user-name to fetch-android.

nixosAndroidBuilder.credentialStorage.credentialDir

Type: absolute path
Default: "/var/lib/credentials"
Directory where credentials are stored. This is the mount point of the dedicated credentials partition and will be bind-mounted to /run/credstore.encrypted/

nixosAndroidBuilder.credentialStorage.enable

Type: boolean
Default: false
Whether to enable persistent credential storage on a TPM2-bound LUKS partition.

nixosAndroidBuilder.credentialStorage.encryptionFlags

Type: list of string
Default: [
  "--with-key=tpm2"
  "--tpm2-pcrs=7"
]
Flags to pass to systemd-creds encrypt. See man (1) systemd-creds

nixosAndroidBuilder.credentialStorage.mountPoint

Type: absolute path
Default: "/run/credstore.encrypted"
Where to mount the credential directory. /run/credstore.encrypted/ is automatically searched by systemd for encrypted credentials

nixosAndroidBuilder.debug

Type: boolean
Default: false
Whether to enable image customizations for interactive access during run-time.

nixosAndroidBuilder.fhsEnv.packages

Type: list of package
Default: [ ]
Packages needed to build Android AOSP. This is mostly copied from AOSP documentation, but could probably be reduced further, as AOSP repos ship much of it in-tree (i.e. python3, jdk, etc)

nixosAndroidBuilder.fhsEnv.pins

Type: list of package
Default: [ ]
A sorted list of packages to add first, so that they “win” if there are collisions/conflicts during creation of the FHS env. Unresolved collisions will produce a warning in the build log.

nixosAndroidBuilder.imageId

Type: string
Default: "android-builder"
The image identifier written to /image-id on the ESP. Defaults to “android-builder” and should not normally need to be changed. Kept separate from system.name so that test VMs can override system.name (which the NixOS test driver uses as the Python machine variable name) without corrupting the image-id that configure-disk-image uses to validate image type.

nixosAndroidBuilder.unattended.enable

Type: boolean
Default: false
Whether to enable unattended mode.

nixosAndroidBuilder.unattended.steps

Type: list of string
Default: [
  "root:start-shell-if-yubikey-found"
  "select-branch"
  "fetch-android"
  "build-android"
  "android-sbom"
  "android-measure-source"
  "copy-android-outputs"
  "root:lock-var-lib-build"
  "root:disable-usb-guard"
  "root:start-shell-and-shutdown"
]
list of shell commands to run unattended

nixosAndroidBuilder.yubikeys.groupA

Type: list of string
Default: [ ]
list of u2f (i.e. yubikeys) public keys for pam, as output by pamu2fcfg
pamu2fcfg -N -i “pam://nixos-android-builder” -o “pam://nixos-android-builder” -u “user”

nixosAndroidBuilder.yubikeys.groupB

Type: list of string
Default: [ ]
list of u2f (i.e. yubikeys) public keys for pam, as output by pamu2fcfg
pamu2fcfg -N -i “pam://nixos-android-builder” -o “pam://nixos-android-builder” -u “user”

services.keylime-agent.enable

Type: boolean
Default: false
Whether to enable Keylime push model agent for TPM-based remote attestation.

services.keylime-agent.logLevel

Type: string
Default: "keylime_push_model_agent=info,keylime=info"
RUST_LOG filter string for the agent. For example "keylime_agent=debug,keylime=info".

services.keylime-agent.package

Type: package
Default: <derivation keylime-agent-0.2.9>
The keylime-agent (Rust) package to use.

services.keylime-agent.settings

Type: attribute set of (string or signed integer or boolean or list of string)
Default: { }
Freeform settings for agent.conf under the [agent] section. Keys should use snake_case INI names matching the Rust keylime-agent config. Values are merged over built-in defaults (rust-keylime v0.2.9).
Note: registrar_ip, verifier_url, and CA cert paths are configured at boot from /boot/attestation-server.json (written by configure-disk-image set-attestation-server). Build-time overrides here will be replaced at runtime if that file exists.

services.keylime.autoEnroll.enable

Type: boolean
Default: false
Whether to enable automatic enrollment of new Keylime agents with measured boot policy.

services.keylime.autoEnroll.enrollPort

Type: 16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)
Default: 8893
HTTPS port for the measured boot report endpoint.

services.keylime.autoEnroll.pollInterval

Type: signed integer
Default: 10
Seconds between polling the registrar for new agents.

services.keylime.enable

Type: boolean
Default: false
Whether to enable Keylime TPM-based remote attestation server.

services.keylime.gitServer.authPort

Type: 16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)
Default: 8895
Localhost port for the attestation auth subrequest backend. Only reachable from nginx on the same host.

services.keylime.gitServer.enable

Type: boolean
Default: false
Whether to enable attestation-gated git HTTP server.

services.keylime.gitServer.port

Type: 16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)
Default: 443
HTTPS port nginx listens on for git clone requests.

services.keylime.gitServer.repoDir

Type: absolute path
Default: "/var/lib/keylime-git/repos"
Directory containing bare git repositories served to attested agents.

services.keylime.gitServer.repos

Type: list of string
Default: [ ]
Bare git repositories to create on first boot (without the .git suffix).

services.keylime.logLevel

Type: one of "DEBUG", "INFO", "WARNING", "ERROR", "CRITICAL"
Default: "INFO"
Log level for keylime services.

services.keylime.logLevelOverrides

Type: attribute set of (one of "DEBUG", "INFO", "WARNING", "ERROR", "CRITICAL")
Default: {
  "keylime.authorization.manager" = "WARNING";
  "keylime.web" = "WARNING";
}
Per-logger log level overrides. Keys are Python logger qualnames (e.g. keylime.web), values are log levels.
By default, the noisy keylime.web and keylime.authorization.manager loggers are set to WARNING to suppress routine per-request INFO messages. Set to { } to restore the previous behaviour.

services.keylime.measuredBootPolicyPath

Type: absolute path
Default: "/nix/store/13bjb1y1nr3w80vksxav2np7bbhkliq0-python3.13-keylime-measured-boot-policy-0.1.0/lib/python3.13/site-packages"
Directory containing the measured boot policy Python module. Added to the verifier’s PYTHONPATH. The module must register a policy name matching measured_boot_policy_name in verifier.conf.

services.keylime.package

Type: package
Default: <derivation keylime-7.14.1-unstable-2026-04-02>
The keylime package to use.

services.keylime.registrar.enable

Type: boolean
Default: false
Whether to enable Keylime registrar service.

services.keylime.registrar.settings

Type: attribute set of (string or signed integer or boolean or list of string or attribute set of (string or signed integer or boolean or list of string))
Default: { }
Settings for registrar.conf [registrar] section.

services.keylime.tenant.settings

Type: attribute set of (string or signed integer or boolean or list of string or attribute set of (string or signed integer or boolean or list of string))
Default: { }
Settings for tenant.conf [tenant] section.

services.keylime.tls.autoGenerate

Type: boolean
Default: true
Automatically generate a self-signed CA and mTLS certificates in /var/lib/keylime/tls/ on first activation. Existing certificates are never overwritten.

services.keylime.tls.certLifetime

Type: signed integer
Default: 365
Validity period in days for generated certificates.

services.keylime.tls.subjectAlternativeNames

Type: list of string
Default: [ ]
Additional Subject Alternative Names for the server certificate. Host IPs are auto-discovered; use this for extra DNS names or IPs.

services.keylime.verifier.enable

Type: boolean
Default: false
Whether to enable Keylime verifier service.

services.keylime.verifier.settings

Type: attribute set of (string or signed integer or boolean or list of string or attribute set of (string or signed integer or boolean or list of string))
Default: { }
Settings for verifier.conf. A nested revocations attrset maps to the [revocations] INI section.

title: 15  Troubleshooting
text:
15.1 Secure Boot
“Secure Boot is disabled” error on boot
Keys won’t enroll
Ensure Setup Mode, not User Mode with existing keys:
Boot fails after key enrollment
Re-sign with the correct keystore and verify:

title: 15  Troubleshooting
text:
Enter firmware setup (F2/F12/Del during POST)
Navigate to Security → Secure Boot → Enable
Save and reboot




Security → Secure Boot → Key Management → Clear All Keys / Reset to Setup Mode
Save and reboot



nix run .#configure-disk-image -- status --keystore ./keys --device <image>

title: 15  Troubleshooting
text:
15.2 Boot
“dm-verity: Hash verification failed” — Store partition corrupted. Rebuild and reflash.
Drops to emergency shell — Check journalctl -e. Common causes: disk too small (needs 250 GB), hardware compatibility.

title: 15  Troubleshooting
text:
15.3 Android Build
fetch-android fails with network errors — For air-gapped environments:
fetch-android --repo-manifest-url=https://your-internal-mirror/platform/manifest
build-android fails with “command not found” — Verify FHS bind mounts:
mount | grep -E '/bin|/lib'
ls -la /bin/bash /lib/ld-linux-x86-64.so.2
Build outputs disappeared after reboot — Enable artifact storage: set nixosAndroidBuilder.artifactStorage.enable = true. See Chapter 4.

title: 15  Troubleshooting
text:
15.4 Attestation
Agent registers but is not enrolled (see Chapter 8 for ports and PKI) — Check journalctl -u keylime-auto-enroll. The daemon waits for both registration AND a measured boot report. Common causes:

report-measured-boot-state service failed (check journalctl -u report-measured-boot-state)
mTLS certificate issues
Port 8893 not reachable

Agent enrolled but fails attestation — Delete enrollment and re-enroll:
keylime_tenant -c delete -u <agent-uuid> -v <verifier-ip> -vp 8881

title: 15  Troubleshooting
text:
15.5 VM Testing
“KVM not available” — Enable VT-x/AMD-V in BIOS. Verify: lsmod | grep kvm
Can’t exit VM — systemctl poweroff or close the QEMU window.

title: 16  Glossary
text:
AK

Attestation Key. A TPM-resident key used by the keylime agent to sign attestation quotes.

AOSP

Android Open Source Project. The publicly available source code for Android maintained by Google.

Attestation

The process of a remote party (verifier) confirming that a machine is running expected software on genuine hardware, based on TPM-signed measurements.

dm-verity

A Linux kernel feature that provides transparent integrity checking of block devices using a Merkle tree.

EFI/UEFI

Unified Extensible Firmware Interface. The modern firmware interface between the operating system and hardware, replacing legacy BIOS.

EK

Endorsement Key. A unique, manufacturer-provisioned key in the TPM that serves as the device’s hardware identity. Used by the keylime agent to derive a stable UUID.

erofs

Enhanced Read-Only File System. A compressed, read-only filesystem used for the immutable store partition.

ESP

EFI System Partition. A FAT-formatted partition containing files needed to boot.

FHS

Filesystem Hierarchy Standard. A standard defining the directory structure and contents of traditional Linux systems (e.g., /bin, /lib, /usr).

Flake

A Nix mechanism for defining reproducible projects with locked dependencies. This project is a flake (see flake.nix).

initrd

Initial RAM Disk. A temporary root filesystem loaded into memory during boot, used to prepare the real root filesystem.

keylime_tenant

Keylime’s upstream CLI tool for enrolling and managing agents with the verifier.

Keylime

An open-source TPM-based remote attestation framework. Consists of an agent, registrar, and verifier.

KVM

Kernel-based Virtual Machine. Linux kernel module for hardware-assisted virtualization.

LUKS

Linux Unified Key Setup. The standard system for Linux disk encryption.

lunch

AOSP’s build configuration selector. Chooses the target product, variant, and build type.

mTLS

Mutual TLS. A TLS connection where both client and server authenticate with certificates. Used between keylime components.

NvPCR

Non-Volatile PCR anchoring. A systemd mechanism (systemd-tpm2-setup.service) that extends PCR 9 at runtime, not captured in the UEFI event log.

PAM

Pluggable Authentication Modules. The Linux framework for pluggable authentication.

pam_u2f

A PAM module for U2F/FIDO2 authentication with hardware tokens such as YubiKeys.

QEMU

A machine emulator and virtualizer. Used for VM-based testing of builder and installer images.

Nix

A purely functional package manager and build system that enables reproducible, declarative builds.

NixOS

A Linux distribution built on Nix, where the entire system configuration is declared in Nix expressions.

nixpkgs

The main repository of Nix packages, containing build instructions for tens of thousands of software packages.

PCR

Platform Configuration Register. A set of SHA-256 registers inside the TPM that accumulate measurements of firmware, boot configuration, and software components. Used for attestation and credential binding.

PK/KEK/DB

Platform Key, Key Exchange Key, and Signature Database. Keys used by UEFI Secure Boot to verify boot components.

repo

Google’s tool for managing multiple Git repositories, used extensively in AOSP development.

SBOM

Software Bill of Materials. A formal inventory of all components and dependencies in a piece of software.

SOPS

Secrets OPerationS. A tool for encrypting/decrypting secrets files, supporting age, GPG, and cloud KMS backends.

SCRTM

Static Root of Trust for Measurement. The initial firmware code that begins the TPM measurement chain at boot.

Secure Boot

A UEFI feature that ensures only cryptographically signed software can be booted.

Setup Mode

A Secure Boot state where custom keys can be enrolled. The firmware accepts new keys without signature verification.

SLSA

Supply-chain Levels for Software Artifacts. A tiered framework for verifying build artifact integrity and provenance.

systemd-creds

A systemd mechanism for encrypting and decrypting credentials, optionally bound to the TPM.

systemd-pcrphase

A systemd service that extends PCR 11 with boot phase strings (sysinit, ready, etc.) at runtime.

systemd-stub

The EFI stub embedded in a Unified Kernel Image. Measures UKI PE sections into PCR 11 and event tags into PCR 9.

systemd-repart

A systemd tool for declaratively creating and managing disk partitions at build time and boot time.

TOFU

Trust on First Use. A security model where the first connection is accepted without verification; subsequent connections are validated against the initial state.

tuigreet

A TUI-based login greeter used by the desktop variant.

TPM

Trusted Platform Module. A dedicated security chip that provides hardware-based cryptographic functions and key storage.

UKI

Unified Kernel Image. A single EFI executable containing the Linux kernel, initrd, and boot parameters.

system-manager

A tool by numtide that applies NixOS-style module configurations to non-NixOS Linux distributions. Used to deploy the attestation server.

USBGuard

A Linux framework for blocking unauthorized USB devices. Used during unattended builds to prevent data exfiltration.

title: 17  Demo Runbook: Attestation-Gated Git
text:
17.1 What We’re Showing
Audience: demo operator (presenting to executives / customers) Format: two machines side by side (~10 min)
See Chapter 9 for the technical reference.
A builder machine can only clone the Android signing-keys repository if its TPM attestation is passing. Revoking via attestation-ctl locks it out instantly — no reboot, no certificate rotation, no firewall change.

title: 17  Demo Runbook: Attestation-Gated Git
text:
17.2 Pre-Demo Prep

17.2.1 1. Populate demo repository on the server
git init --bare /var/lib/keylime-git/repos/android-signing-keys.git
# seed with realistic content (README + dummy keys)
git -C /var/lib/keylime-git/repos/android-signing-keys.git update-server-info
ln -s /etc/keylime-git/hooks/post-receive \
      /var/lib/keylime-git/repos/android-signing-keys.git/hooks/post-receive


17.2.2 2. Verify agent credentials
Client cert and key are written to /run/keylime-git/ by report-measured-boot-state. The keylime-git-clone wrapper configures git automatically.
Preflight:
keylime-git-clone https://<server-ip>/android-signing-keys.git /tmp/preflight
ls /tmp/preflight/keys/
rm -rf /tmp/preflight


17.2.3 3. Terminal layout
Server — tmux, 3 panes:

Top-left: ready for attestation-ctl
Top-right: journalctl -fu keylime-git-auth (live ALLOW/DENY stream)
Bottom: ready for attestation-ctl remove <uuid>

Agent — one clean pane at ~.


17.2.4 4. Checklist (30 min before)

Agent booted, attestation passing (attestation-ctl status shows PASS)
Preflight clone succeeds
journalctl -fu keylime-git-auth running
Both screens visible

title: 17  Demo Runbook: Attestation-Gated Git
text:
17.3 Live Script

17.3.1 Act 1 — The problem (1 min, verbal)

“This builder produces Android images signed with production keys. Those keys live in a git repo. How do you make sure only a legitimate, unmodified builder gets them?”



17.3.2 Act 2 — Attestation status (2 min)
attestation-ctl status

“The server continuously verifies this machine’s firmware, bootloader, and OS via the TPM. It passed 12 seconds ago.”



17.3.3 Act 3 — Attested clone (2 min)
On the agent:
keylime-git-clone https://<server-ip>/android-signing-keys.git
ls android-signing-keys/keys/
Server log shows: ALLOW aabbccdd-… — agent is attested


17.3.4 Act 4 — Revocation (3 min)
On the server:
attestation-ctl remove <uuid>
On the agent:
keylime-git-clone https://<server-ip>/android-signing-keys.git /tmp/second
# fatal: 403
Server log shows: DENY aabbccdd-… — agent not enrolled

“Same machine. Same certificate. Only the attestation status changed.”



17.3.5 Act 5 — Q&A (2 min)







Question
Answer




Stolen certificate?
Server checks live attestation status on every request.


Faked TPM / VM?
EK certificate verified against manufacturer CA.


How often re-checked?
Every git request; verifier re-attests every 2 seconds.


Can the builder push?
No — read-only from agent side.

title: 17  Demo Runbook: Attestation-Gated Git
text:
17.4 Recovery
Re-enroll after demo:
systemctl restart keylime-auto-enroll   # on server
# wait ~30 seconds
attestation-ctl status

title: 18  Contributing
text: 18.1 Quick Reference

title: 18  Contributing
text:
Task
Command




Build disk image
nix build .#image


Run builder VM
nix run .#run-vm


Run desktop VM (GNOME)
nix run .#run-desktop-gnome-vm


Run all checks
nix flake check


Format code
nix fmt


Enter dev shell
nix develop


Build this manual
nix run .#build-book

title: 18  Contributing
text:
18.2 Development Workflow

Edit modules in modules/
Build: nix build .#image
Test in VM: nix run .#run-vm
Run checks: nix flake check
Format: nix fmt

title: 18  Contributing
text:
18.3 Tests
NixOS VM tests verify the build environment, Secure Boot, dm-verity, installer, and attestation:
nix build -L .#checks.x86_64-linux.integration
nix build -L .#checks.x86_64-linux.installer
nix build -L .#checks.x86_64-linux.keylime
nix build -L .#checks.x86_64-linux.keylime-auto-enroll
Tests are only re-run when inputs change. Pass --keep-vm-state to preserve VM state for iterative debugging.

title: 18  Contributing
text:
18.4 Project Structure
flake.nix                     # Flake definition
configuration.nix             # Builder config (site-specific)
desktop-configuration.nix     # Desktop config (site-specific)

modules/                      # NixOS modules
├── image.nix                 # Disk image layout (largest module)
├── android-build-env.nix     # AOSP build toolchain
├── fhsenv.nix                # FHS compatibility layer
├── keylime.nix               # Keylime server
├── keylime-agent.nix         # Keylime agent
├── secure-boot.nix           # Secure Boot / UKI
├── credential-storage.nix    # TPM credential storage
├── yubikey-auth.nix          # YubiKey PAM
├── unattended.nix            # Unattended build pipeline
└── ...

packages/                     # Custom Nix packages
system-manager/               # Keylime server for non-NixOS
tests/                        # NixOS integration tests
docs/                         # This manual (Quarto)

title: 18  Contributing
text:
18.5 Security Notes

Never commit keys — keys/ is gitignored
UKI signing runs outside Nix to avoid leaking keys into /nix/store
Debug mode gives unauthenticated root — production must disable it
